professional_development.professional_development_major_workshops:
Summer 2023 – Ignatian Pedagogy and Spirituality Workshop (Jesuit Schools Network, Portland, OR)
Week-long intensive focused on integrating Ignatian pedagogy into secondary education. Participated in daily sessions on the Ignatian Pedagogical Paradigm (context, experience, reflection, action, evaluation), led small-group reflections, and developed a year-long plan to incorporate Ignatian examen practices into my sophomore English and Journalism courses. Completed a capstone project designing a cross-curricular unit on social justice in media, which I later implemented with my students.

Fall 2022 – Culturally Responsive Teaching in Secondary Classrooms (Lewis & Clark Graduate School, 3-credit continuing education course)
Evening course over 10 weeks focused on equity, inclusion, and culturally sustaining pedagogy. Conducted an equity audit of my classroom curriculum, revised reading lists to include more authors of color and voices from the Global South, and implemented new discussion protocols to ensure equitable participation. Final project included a detailed action plan for building inclusive classroom norms and addressing microaggressions.

Summer 2022 – High School Journalism Advisers Workshop (University of Oregon School of Journalism and Communication)
Five-day residential workshop for journalism advisers. Sessions included media law and ethics, advising student newspapers, digital storytelling, and newsroom management. I developed a new editorial policy handbook for our school paper, trained student editors in fact-checking procedures, and created a plan for transitioning our print paper to a hybrid print/online model.

Spring 2021 – Online and Hybrid Learning Design Institute (Jesuit High School Professional Learning Community)
Internal but intensive 8-week institute created during the pandemic to improve online and hybrid instruction. Focused on backward design, asynchronous engagement strategies, and effective feedback in digital environments. I redesigned my sophomore English course in Canvas, created video mini-lessons, and built rubric-based feedback templates to streamline grading while maintaining depth of commentary.

Summer 2020 – Trauma-Informed Practices in the Classroom (Oregon Education Association, 2-day virtual institute)
Professional learning focused on understanding the impact of trauma on adolescent brain development and behavior, particularly in the context of COVID-19 and social unrest. Learned specific strategies for de-escalation, relationship-building, and creating predictable routines. Implemented weekly check-in circles and anonymous feedback forms in my classes to better track student well-being.

Fall 2019 – AP English Language and Composition Summer Institute (College Board, Seattle, WA)
Four-day AP Summer Institute preparing teachers to teach AP English Language and Composition. Deep dive into exam structure, scoring rubrics, and best practices for teaching rhetorical analysis and argumentation. I developed a full-year pacing guide, unit plans, and formative assessments aligned with College Board expectations, which I have since refined and used each year with my AP students.
professional_development.professional_development_additional_entr:
Winter 2023 – Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in Catholic Schools Seminar (Archdiocese of Portland, 4-session series)
Monthly evening seminar series focused on the intersection of Catholic identity and DEI work. Explored Church documents on social justice, the dignity of the human person, and solidarity. Participated in case studies on navigating difficult conversations around race, gender, and identity in a Catholic school context. As a final project, I co-developed a proposal for a student-led Diversity Council and a series of advisory lessons on belonging and allyship, which our school began piloting in Fall 2023.

Spring 2022 – Student Mental Health First Aid Certification (National Council for Mental Wellbeing)
One-day certification course training educators to recognize and respond to signs of mental health and substance use challenges in adolescents. Learned how to use the ALGEE action plan (Approach, Listen, Give support, Encourage professional help, Encourage self-help). Since completing the training, I have served as a point person in our English department for connecting students to counseling resources and have incorporated stress-management mini-lessons before major projects.

Summer 2019 – Project-Based Learning in the Humanities Institute (Buck Institute for Education, virtual)
Three-day workshop on designing rigorous project-based learning units in English and Social Studies. I designed a “Community Voices Oral History Project” in which students interviewed local community members, transcribed and edited their stories, and compiled them into a digital anthology. The project culminated in an evening event where students presented their work to families and interviewees.

Ongoing 2020–2024 – Professional Learning Community: Reading Apprenticeship in Secondary English (Jesuit High School)
Bi-monthly PLC focused on improving students’ disciplinary literacy skills. We studied Reading Apprenticeship strategies, practiced think-alouds, and co-designed reading routines for complex texts. I piloted metacognitive logs with my sophomores, tracked their reading confidence over time, and shared data with the PLC to refine our approach.

Spring 2023 – Grant Recipient: Classroom Innovation Mini-Grant (Jesuit High School Advancement Office)
Received a $1,200 internal mini-grant to create a small classroom podcasting studio for my Journalism and English classes. Wrote the proposal, researched cost-effective equipment, and implemented a podcasting project in which students produced narrative podcasts on local social justice issues. Assessed student learning in research, interviewing, audio editing, and reflective writing.

Fall 2021 – Workshop Series: Coaching for Character in High School Athletics (Oregon School Activities Association)
Three-part evening workshop series for coaches on integrating character education, sportsmanship, and leadership development into athletic programs. Learned specific frameworks for teaching resilience, growth mindset, and team accountability. Applied these strategies as an assistant coach for girls’ soccer, incorporating weekly leadership reflections and goal-setting sessions into our practice schedule.

references.reference_1_relationship: English Department Chair and Direct Supervisor (current)
references.reference_2_relationship: Campus Ministry Collaborator; Co-leader on Christian Service Immersion Trips
references.reference_3_relationship: Former Principal at Lincoln High School; Previous Supervisor (2016–2020)
